_metadata.source: FAO Fertilizer Guide, IRRI nutrient interaction research
_metadata.description: Optimal nutrient ratios and pH-availability thresholds

n_p_ratio.description: N:P ratio — outside range indicates P is limiting (Liebig's law)

n_k_ratio.description: N:K ratio — K deficiency if ratio too high

nitrogen.note: N availability drops sharply below pH 5.5

phosphorus.note: P fixation increases above pH 7.5 and below pH 5.5

potassium.note: K availability relatively stable across pH range

zinc.note: Zn availability decreases 100x for each pH unit above 7.0

boron.note: B leaching risk in acidic sandy soils

antagonisms.effect: Excess P reduces Zn uptake — common in DAP-heavy programs
Lime application can reduce K availability
Excess N without K causes lodging in rice/sugarcane
